outcome_text: A determination is made as to whether the AI system achieves its intended purpose and stated objectives and whether its development or deployment should proceed.
scoring_text: Skill in AI system go/no-go determination, including evaluating whether an AI system achieves its intended purpose against stated objectives, conducting readiness assessments for development progression or deployment, and making evidence-based proceed/halt decisions.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in AI system go/no-go determination, including evaluating whether an AI system achieves its intended purpose against stated objectives, conducting readiness assessments for development progression or deployment, and making evidence-based proceed/halt decisions.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in communicating AI test results to technical and non-technical stakeholders, including limitation documentation and clear go/no-go recommendations.
comments: NIST AI RMF MG-1.1 (MANAGE) superset of WIDAI RM domain. RM-S-005 is the strongest match (STS: 0.646, cosine: 0.679). Secondary: AI-S-018 (AI, cosine: 0.592), RM-T-003 (RM, cosine: 0.552).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.5/10 (raw 0.646). Shared vocabulary: [ai, go, skill]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [achieves, against, assessments, based, conducting, decisions] vs WIDAI terms [clear, communicating, documentation, limitation, non, recommendations] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.877 (P=0.869, R=0.885). Balanced P/R — the 32-word competency implication and 18-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the NIST AI RMF element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.679 ≈ STS 0.646 (gap 0.033). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [ai, go, skill] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 15.9%. Shared tokens: [ai, go, skill]. NIST AI RMF-only: [achieves, against, assessments, based, conducting, decisions]. WIDAI-only: [clear, communicating, documentation, limitation, non, recommendations].
